dailyJudgementSnippet: Anthropic won because Claude Code was mentioned across multiple front-page threads as a practical tool for analysis, Linux customization, and UI generation, giving it the broadest and cleanest positive HN signal of the day [E1][E2][E3].
winnerExplanation: Anthropic had the strongest mix of coverage and tone: Claude Code anchored a Show HN project, was praised for making Linux setup tweaks easy, and was cited as capable of turning ASCII or tmux-style layouts into working interfaces. The main pushback was skepticism about one demo's output quality, not a broad rejection of Claude itself, so the overall read stayed clearly favorable [E1][E2][E3].

openai.judgementSnippet: ChatGPT Health was the only clear OpenAI-specific signal: the title was skeptical, but comments split between strong reports of health-related usefulness and worries about misuse or refusals, leaving the day mixed rather than clearly up or down [E4].

anthropic.judgementSnippet: Claude Code kept showing up as a practical tool: it anchored a Show HN project, was praised for making Linux setup tweaks fast, and was cited as able to turn ASCII or tmux sketches into working interfaces, even with some skepticism about one demo's output quality [E1][E2][E3].

google_gemini.judgementSnippet: Gemini benefited from favorable direct comparisons in health and coding discussions, with commenters calling it cooperative on symptom and treatment questions and capable of turning ASCII or tmux sketches into working layouts [E5][E6].

evidence.summary: Show HN credited Claude Code for mapping connections across 100 books; comments included another user using Claude Code to inspect GitHub projects, though one commenter questioned whether the generated groupings were meaningful.
In the Linux migration discussion, a top comment said Claude Code makes personalizing, adapting, and automating a Linux setup 'very fast and trivial.'
A top comment said agents are good at driving tmux and that prompting Claude with ASCII or TUI layouts can produce working versions, calling it 'space alien technology.'

The title framed ChatGPT Health as extractive, but comments were mixed: one user described major help with rehab planning and communicating with doctors, while others warned about misuse or refusals on health questions.
In the ChatGPT Health thread, commenters said they rebuilt a rehab program with ChatGPT and Gemini and specifically found Gemini more cooperative on symptoms and treatments.
A top comment praised prompting Gemini with ASCII or tmux layouts and getting back working versions.
